examples.title: Symbolic Constant with #define
examples.description: Demonstrates defining constants using #define preprocessor directive
examples.explanation: The #define directive creates macro substitutions. PI, MAX_STUDENTS, and PRINT_MSG are replaced by their values before compilation. This is a compile-time constant with no memory allocation.

examples.title: Constant Variable with const Keyword
examples.description: Shows use of const keyword for creating typed constants
examples.explanation: The const keyword creates variables with fixed values. These are type-checked, have scope rules, and allocate memory. Attempting to modify them after initialization causes a compilation error.

examples.title: Enumeration Constants Demo
examples.description: Demonstrates defining and using enumeration constants in C
examples.explanation: Enums assign symbolic names to integer constants. By default, the first value is 0 unless specified. Values increment by 1 from the previous assigned value. GREEN=5 makes BLUE=6.

examples.title: Const with Pointers - Three Variants
examples.description: Demonstrates different ways const can be used with pointers
examples.explanation: Three variants provide different levels of protection: (1) const int *ptr - pointer to constant data, (2) int *const ptr - constant pointer, (3) const int *const ptr - both constant. Each prevents different kinds of modifications.
